# Bitcoin Dev Kit (BDK)

> A modern, lightweight, descriptor-based wallet library written in Rust.

## Project Overview

The Bitcoin Dev Kit (BDK) is a suite of libraries for building modern Bitcoin wallets and applications. It provides well-engineered components for descriptor-based wallets, chain data management, and blockchain data sources. Built on `rust-bitcoin` and `rust-miniscript`.

## Architecture

Rust workspace with modular crates in `/crates/` and example applications in `/examples/`.

### Workspace Crates

| Crate | Path | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `bdk_chain` | `crates/chain/` | Tools for storing and indexing chain data (keychain tracking, tx graph) |
| `bdk_core` | `crates/core/` | Core structures shared across BDK crates (spk indices, tx updates) |
| `bdk_file_store` | `crates/file_store/` | Append-only flat file database for persisting bdk_chain data |
| `bdk_electrum` | `crates/electrum/` | Electrum blockchain data source for BDK |
| `bdk_esplora` | `crates/esplora/` | Esplora blockchain data source for BDK |
| `bdk_bitcoind_rpc` | `crates/bitcoind_rpc/` | Bitcoin Core RPC blockchain data source |
| `bdk_testenv` | `crates/testenv/` | Testing framework for BDK chain sources |

### Example Applications

| Example | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `example_cli` | Minimal wallet using `bdk_wallet` |
| `example_electrum` | Wallet backed by Electrum server |
| `example_esplora` | Wallet backed by Esplora block explorer |
| `example_bitcoind_rpc_polling` | Wallet using Bitcoin Core RPC polling |

## Key Concepts

- **Descriptors**: BDK uses output descriptors (miniscript) for wallet key management
- **Chain sources**: Pluggable blockchain data backends (Electrum, Esplora, bitcoind RPC)
- **Keychain tracking**: Per-keychain derivation index tracking for address discovery
- **Tx graph**: In-memory transaction graph with confirmation tracking
- **Persistence**: Optional append-only file store for wallet state

## Key Dependencies

- `rust-bitcoin` — Bitcoin protocol types and primitives
- `rust-miniscript` — Descriptor and miniscript support
- `electrum-client` — Electrum protocol client
- `esplora-client` — Esplora block explorer client
- `tokio` — Async runtime (for RPC chain sources)

## Building and Running

```bash
# Build all crates
cargo build

# Run examples
cargo run --example example_cli
cargo run --example example_esplora

# Run tests
cargo test

# Run with specific feature
cargo test --features "electrum"
```

Requires Rust 1.85.0+ (see `rust-version` file).

## Testing

```bash
# Unit tests
cargo test

# Integration tests (requires test environment)
cargo test --features test-env

# Check clippy
cargo clippy --all-targets
```

Uses `bdk_testenv` crate for integration testing against real Bitcoin Core instances.

## Contributing

- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — Contribution guidelines
- `DEVELOPMENT_CYCLE.md` — Release cycle documentation
- Discord: https://discord.gg/d7NkDKm
- Licensed: MIT OR Apache-2.0

## Code Style

- `rustfmt.toml` — Rust formatting rules
- `clippy.toml` — Clippy lint configuration
- Workspace-level lints deny `print_stdout` and `print_stderr`

## Important Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Cargo.toml` | Workspace definition |
| `justfile` | Task runner recipes |
| `ci/` | CI/CD workflows |
| `docs/` | Additional documentation |
| `nursery/` | Experimental/prototype code |

## Notes

- BDK has an existing `AGENTS.md` in `rust-bitcoin/rust-bitcoin` (upstream dependency) requiring AI commit attribution
- The project uses a structured release cycle (see `DEVELOPMENT_CYCLE.md`)
- `nursery/` contains experimental code not ready for production use
